page: Amazon Web Services m2.4xlarge is a memory-optimized second-generation virtual server instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2665 0 processor operating at 2.4 GHz. The system is configured with 68.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.55 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1680 GB hard disk drive (HDD), and network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the m2.4xlarge exhibits modest processing capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories respectively. Multi-core performance is slightly stronger, achieving average ratings in stress-ng multi-core tests and software compilation benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains stable, scoring in the average tier for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. However, cryptographic and text processing performance is poor, reflecting the lack of modern instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES-NI.

From a resource density perspective, the m2.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on legacy hardware. The inclusion of a large 1680 GB local HDD provides substantial storage capacity, though it lacks the speed of modern solid-state drives. This server is best suited for legacy memory-bound workloads, basic database operations, and batch processing tasks that require significant RAM but do not demand high-frequency CPU performance or modern cryptographic acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m2.4xlarge is a legacy memory-optimized virtual server running on the Xen hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2665 0 processor at 2.4 GHz, paired with 68.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1680 GB local HDD. Lacking modern CPU instruction sets like AVX and AES, its single-core compute and cryptographic performance fall into the lower percentiles. However, it maintains average multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. With a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is designed for memory-intensive legacy applications, database operations, and basic batch processing that benefit from high memory density rather than raw compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs, 68 GB of RAM, and 1680 GB of local HDD storage, designed specifically for legacy memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m2.4xlarge is an 8-vCPU virtual server with 68 GB RAM and 1680 GB local HDD storage for memory-intensive legacy workloads.
tagline: A legacy memory-optimized virtual server featuring high RAM density and large local HDD storage for basic database and batch workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2665 0 processor at 2.4 GHz
68.0 GB of system memory providing 8.55 GB of RAM per core
1680 GB of bundled local HDD storage for high-capacity data needs
Average performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth tests
Suited for legacy memory-bound applications and basic database operations
